ques: Locate a repository on GitHub that was created in the last week and has 50 or more stars. Provide brief details about the project's purpose and its programming language.
answer: overflowy/make-look-scanned — a CLI tool (also runnable in-browser via WebAssembly) that degrades a PDF to look like a physical scan, adding skew, grayscale, warm paper tone, scanner grain, defocus, edge shadow, and JPEG artifacts to produce an image-only PDF. Created in the last week with 415 stars; written primarily in Go (67.5%).
